sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 52

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 52:1
items.html:
(to the question) Chatzi Shiur Vav [half the measure of the Vav]
Tifferet, which is CHaGaT, is called a Vav with a head [Rosh], being a level of Bechina Aleph, in which there are CHaGaT NHY — where the CHaGaT are the discernment of KaCHaB of Hasadim [mercies], and hence are the discernment of a Rosh, and the NHY in it are the discernment of a Guf [body]. But Yesod, which is a level of NHY alone and lacks the discernment of CHaGaT, is discerned as a cut-off Vav, which has no head; for it lacks the discernment of the five Hasadim of CHaGaT, and has only the discernment of their collectivity alone, which is the level of Malchut alone, as is known. It is likewise discerned as half the measure of the great Vav, for the NHY are half the Partzuf of ZA, which comprises the six extremities. (p. 747, Item 20)
